description: 20-question recall quiz for the ml-platform fixture. Supersede pairs under test: serving (vLLM → TGI) and vector DB (pgvector → Qdrant).

questions.question: What currently serves LLM inference?
questions.groundTruth: Hugging Face TGI. It replaced vLLM because TGI's LoRA-adapter-per-request matched our multi-tenant routing (vLLM's multi-LoRA was experimental), streaming + token-logprobs API fit product requirements, and quantization stability (GPTQ/AWQ) was better.

questions.question: Where do RAG embeddings currently live?
questions.groundTruth: Qdrant (self-hosted cluster on GKE). It replaced pgvector after crossing 20M vectors: pgvector latency at the required recall was 200-400ms while Qdrant is sub-50ms; Qdrant's payload filtering scales linearly; horizontal scaling is first-class.

questions.question: Was serving always on TGI?
questions.groundTruth: No. Originally vLLM. Superseded by TGI primarily because of multi-tenant LoRA-per-request support. The vLLM decision is marked superseded.

questions.question: Was vector search always on Qdrant?
questions.groundTruth: No. Originally pgvector on Cloud SQL. Superseded by Qdrant at ~20M vectors for latency and horizontal-scale reasons. Postgres remains the source-of-truth for text chunks; Qdrant holds vectors + payload.

questions.question: Why Argo Workflows over Kubeflow Pipelines for training orchestration?
questions.groundTruth: Kubeflow Pipelines pulls in an opinionated Kubeflow install; Metaflow assumes AWS and we're on GKE. Argo is a plain Kubernetes CRD so we reuse our RBAC and node-pool scheduling.

questions.question: Why self-hosted MLflow instead of W&B for experiment tracking?
questions.groundTruth: Customer-data compliance blocks sending run metadata to W&B servers. MLflow's artifact store points at GCS; metadata DB is Cloud SQL Postgres. Every run must log `lora_r`, `lora_alpha`, `eval_loss`, `rouge_l`.

questions.question: Why A100 over H100 GPUs?
questions.groundTruth: H100 availability was poor when we signed the reserved-capacity contract, H100 list price was ~2.5x A100, and LoRA fine-tuning (dominant workload) rarely hits the A100's memory ceiling.

questions.question: Why LoRA adapters over full fine-tuning for customer-specific models?
questions.groundTruth: Adapters are ~100MB vs tens of GB for full models (cheap per-tenant storage); TGI hot-swaps adapters per request; LoRA training converges in hours on a single A100. Full fine-tuning is reserved for the base model.

questions.question: A contributor proposes serving a new customer tenant via vLLM 'for throughput'. What's the call?
questions.groundTruth: Reject. Serving is TGI — the multi-tenant LoRA-per-request requirement is what drove the supersede. Add the tenant's LoRA to TGI's adapter routing.

questions.question: A PR adds a pgvector HNSW index for a new embeddings table. Align?
questions.groundTruth: No. Vector search is Qdrant; pgvector was superseded. Add the collection to Qdrant with appropriate payload fields for tenant filtering.

questions.question: A new model version is ready. How is promotion to Production gated?
questions.groundTruth: Via MLflow's model registry. Requirements: eval metrics within 5% of previous prod, manual approve in the registry UI, and a 24h shadow canary before full cutover. Models not in the registry are not served.

questions.question: I need to kick off a training run on a customer dataset tagged pii_present=true. What must happen?
questions.groundTruth: Must use Opacus (DP-SGD) with per-customer epsilon budget. Budget is enforced at job-submit time — if remaining epsilon is less than required, the job refuses to start. Never train on PII-tagged data without DP enabled.

questions.question: I'm adding inference for a new model family. What quantization does production use, and why?
questions.groundTruth: AWQ int4 (Activation-aware Weight Quantization). GPTQ was slightly lower quality in our A/B; bitsandbytes int8 was worse throughput on A100. Regression is measured on a 2k-prompt eval set before promotion. Never serve un-quantized in production.

questions.question: Where do I get feature values at inference time?
questions.groundTruth: Feast online store (Redis on GKE). Never read features directly from BigQuery at inference — always via Feast.

questions.question: Why GKE over EC2/EKS for training infra?
questions.groundTruth: A100 availability in us-central1 was consistent while EC2 P4 had 6-week waits during our ramp. GKE Autopilot was rejected because we need fine-grained GPU node-pool control. Spot VMs for short jobs (<1h), on-demand for long runs.

questions.question: Why Feast over Tecton for the feature store?
questions.groundTruth: Tecton was too expensive for our feature-row volume; rolling our own was maintenance burden. Feast's online store is Redis on GKE; offline is BigQuery. Feature views live in features/*.py applied via `feast apply` in CI.

questions.question: Why PyTorch over JAX?
questions.groundTruth: Team's PyTorch muscle memory is deeper; HuggingFace ecosystem (Transformers, PEFT, TRL) is PyTorch-native; torch.compile closed most of the JAX perf gap. No TensorFlow anywhere.

questions.question: What's the format for training data and how is versioning handled?
questions.groundTruth: Parquet on GCS loaded via webdataset. Versioning is by content hash — never mutate in place. Parquet was chosen over TFRecord (TF-centric) and JSONL (10x size).

questions.question: Why Evidently AI for model monitoring?
questions.groundTruth: WhyLabs is SaaS-only for the tier we wanted; Arize was expensive at our prediction volume. Evidently reports generate nightly via Argo, published to a Grafana iframe. Drift alerts feed PagerDuty with 2-hour dedup.

questions.question: A PR wants to run fine-tuning CI on GitHub-hosted GPU runners. OK?
questions.groundTruth: No. CI uses self-hosted GPU runners (A10s, not A100s) in a dedicated GKE node pool via Actions Runner Controller — runner-minute pricing on hosted GPU services exceeds our steady-state self-hosted cost.
